I treated the prior turns as proposals, not as proof. The prompt fixes the tame-format presentation with distinguished sigma,tau, implicit tau^sigma=tau^2, later generators in the pro-2 core, and exponents reduced modulo M=85667662080.The only candidate in the prior log that has not been refuted by the recorded finite-quotient tests is still the dynamic tame-inertia word from Turns 9‚Äď48.

The live candidate remains:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with ŌÄ=ŌÄ2‚ąąZ and ő∑=2ŌÄ on odd tame inertia,r=xŌÉ‚ąíŌÄxŌÉŌÄx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ ((yŌĄ)ŌÄ)ŌÉy‚ąí1,where ŌÄ‚Č°40491355905,‚ąíŌÄ‚Č°45176306175,‚ąíő∑‚Č°50932630783(modM).The external benchmark remains the same: the known Jannsen‚ÄďWingberg strategy is formation-theoretic over finite tame subextensions, not just a rank or abstract DemuŇ°kin-kernel check; Neukirch‚Äôs report describes this explicit determination through tame subextensions and DemuŇ°kin data, while Jarden‚ÄďShusterman record finite presentability and the local generator/relation counts.Numdam+1 Labute‚Äôs classification is the right comparison point for the dyadic pro-2 kernels and their orientation/root-of-unity invariant.Cambridge University Press & Assessment The main correction this turn is that Turn 48 still had the trivial-block quotient reversed. The sign-cover formula in Turn 48 is correct, but the trivial ŌĄ-block formula is not.For the sign-cover calculation, let V=kerőļ, let t be the deck involution, and restrict to a nontrivial odd tame-inertia character block ŌĄ‚Ü¶őĪÓÄ†=1. ForNa (u)=(uŌĄa)ŌÄ,the Fox coefficient isDa=j=0‚ąĎŌÄ‚ąí1(tőĪa)j.

Since (1‚ąítőĪa)Da=1‚ąí(tőĪa)ŌÄ and (tőĪa)ŌÄ=t, the correct expression isDa=(1‚ąít)(1‚ąítőĪa)‚ąí1.

This is the direction that makes the argument work: 1‚ąítőĪa is a 2-adic unit, while 1‚ąít is radical. Hence Da is radical, so the coefficient contributed by x‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ is ‚ąí1+D‚ąíő∑, a unit. Thus nontrivial odd tame-character sign-cover blocks still contribute only unit Smith pivots.But in the trivial tame-inertia block, with T the ŌÉ-cycle operator and s‚Č°ŌÄ(modf), the mod-2 relation is(1+T‚ąís)X+(1+T)Y=0.

Write f=2a m with m odd. Then s‚Č°1(mod2a) and s‚Č°0(modm). If f is even, s is odd, so 1+T‚ą£1+T‚ąís.

Therefore the valid elimination isY=Ps (T)X+YňČ,Ps(T)=1+T1+T‚ąís,up to the harmless convention T‚ÜĒT‚ąí1. The reciprocal (1+T)/(1+T‚ąís) is not legitimate in F2 [T]/(Tf‚ąí1).

With the corrected Ps,h(T)=1+Ps (T)+Ps(T‚ąí1)satisfies, for ő∂f=1, ő∂ÓÄ†=1,(1+ő∂)h(ő∂)=ő∂‚ąís+ő∂s+1=ő∂‚ąís(1+ő∂2s+1).Since 2s+1 is odd and 2s+1‚Č°1(modm), gcd(f,2s+1)=1. Thus h has no nontrivial f-th-root zero, and h(1)=1. The trivial-block nonsingularity proof survives, but only withPs(T)=1+T1+T‚ąís.A useful sanity check is f=4,s=1: the corrected formula gives Ps=T‚ąí1, i.e. Yg=YňČ+XgŌÉ‚ąí1, which is exactly the linear solution used in the successful low-degree computations. The reversed quotient would give T, which is the wrong shift.With these corrected formulas, the prior finite-tame abelianization conclusions remain consistent:Ue,fab‚ČÉZ2ef+1‚äēC2,and, for the sign kernel,(kerőļ)ab‚ČÉZ22ef+1‚äēC4.The new structural contribution is a general version of the mixed-tame IA tower that prior turns isolated only in the S3 case.LetőĒe,f=‚ü®ŌÉňČ,ŌĄňČ‚ą£ŌĄňČe=1,ŌÉňČf=1,ŌÉňČŌĄňČŌÉňČ‚ąí1=ŌĄňČ2‚ü©,e¬†odd,¬†e‚ą£2f‚ąí1.

Write f=2a m, m odd, and let

H=‚ü®ŌĄňČ,ŌÉňČ2a‚ü©,P=őĒe,f/H‚ČÉC2a.

In the reduced first graded piece,L1‚ČÉF2[őĒe,f]‚äēF2 b‚äēF2S,with marked S=ŌÉf, the quadratic DemuŇ°kin initial form isq=g‚ąąőĒe,f‚ąĎXg[2]+[b,S].The first marked IA ambiguity after odd-Hall rigidity reduces toAe,f‚ČÉH1(P,L2H),whereL2=(L1[2]‚äē‚ąß2L1)/‚ü®q‚ü©.

Turn 48‚Äôs dimension formula can be strengthened as follows. For f even, the nonfree part of L2His(L2H)nonfree‚ČÉJ1‚äē2‚äēJ2 a‚ąí1‚äēI(e,f),I(e,f)=gcd(e,2f/2+1),where Jr denotes the unipotent indecomposable F2[C2a]-module of length r. The two J1 summands are the universal square directions b[2]and S[2]. The J2a‚ąí1 -summands are indexed by the involutionsőīj=ŌĄňČjŌÉňČf/2,j(1+2f/2)‚Č°0(mode).

For each such involution, defineZj=h‚ąąH‚ąĎ[Xh,Xhőīj].

Then Zj is the degree-two mixed-tame direction attached to őīj. More strongly, it has a whole restricted-power tower of relation-preserving tangent classes. For n‚Č•0, setTj,n=(adZj)2n‚ąí1(S),with Tj,0=S, and defineőīj,n (b)=Zj[2n],őīj,n(Xh)=[Xhőīj,Tj,n],őīj,n(Xhőīj)=[Xh,Tj,n],őīj,n (S)=0 for h‚ąąH, with zero on the other P-cosets for this displayed representative.

Then őīj,n preserves the initial DemuŇ°kin relation to first order:őīj,n (q)=0.

Indeed, the contribution from the X[2]-part is h‚ąąH‚ąĎ([Xh,[Xhőīj,Tj,n]]+[Xhőīj,[Xh,Tj,n]])=[Zj,Tj,n],and the contribution from [b,S] is[Zj[2n],S].

The restricted-Lie identity(adZj)2n=ad(Zj[2n])gives[Zj,Tj,n]=[Zj[2n],S],so the two terms cancel in characteristic 2.

This generalizes the prior S3 calculation. In (e,f)=(3,2), the three involutions ŌĄj ŌÉ give the three classes Z0,Z1,Z2. In (e,f)=(5,4), the five involutions ŌĄjŌÉ2 give five mixed directions, plus the two universal square directions, hence the seven-dimensional first IA ambiguity found in Turn 48. The next arithmetic comparison should therefore not be an enormous operator-matrix comparison; it should extract exactly these seven coordinates in the (5,4) case.The live candidate is still not proved. The corrected algebra preserves the finite-tame abelian, cup-product, sign-kernel, and cyclotomic consistency checks in the prior log, but the remaining gap is genuinely filtered-action-theoretic: one must show that the exact (ŌÉ,ŌĄ)-operator action selected by the candidate realizes the arithmetic action, including the involution-indexed mixed towers above.

ESTABLISHED: The dynamic candidate remains unrefuted. Turn 48‚Äôs sign-cover formula Da=(1‚ąít)(1‚ąítőĪa)‚ąí1 is correct, but its trivial-block quotient was reversed. The valid elimination isPs(T)=1+T1+T‚ąís,up to T‚ÜĒT‚ąí1. With this correction, the prior conclusionsUe,fab‚ČÉZ2ef+1‚äēC2,(kerőļ)ab‚ČÉZ22ef+1‚äēC4 remain consistent. I also generalized the mixed-tame first IA ambiguity: each involution ŌĄjŌÉf/2 yields an explicit tower of relation-preserving tangent classes őīj,n with őīj,n (b)=Zj[2n].

GAP: This is still not a proof of the presentation of GQ2. The missing ingredient is a dyadic formation/rigidity theorem, or an explicit arithmetic comparison, proving that the candidate‚Äôs exact filtered tame action realizes the actual arithmetic Frobenius/tame-inertia action for all finite tame subextensions.

NEXT: Compute the candidate‚Äôs actual coordinates in the two universal square directions and the five involution-indexed directions for (e,f)=(5,4), then compare those seven coordinates with explicit Kummer/Witt arithmetic for the corresponding tame degree-20 extension.[BREAKTHROUGH]
